title: Add TUI Responsiveness Regression Coverage
description: Extend TUI regression coverage to lock in manual-vs-auto refresh behavior, scroll interactions, mouse routing, incremental update expectations where testable, and no-regression safety behavior for merge, retry, status updates, and feature-root scoping.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover default manual refresh behavior and explicit continuous or timed refresh enablement.
Tests cover keyboard and mouse scrolling behavior for list and detail panels, including selection reset semantics for detail view.
Tests cover deterministic mouse click and wheel routing for list versus detail interactions.
Tests preserve existing safety-path expectations for retry, merge, status confirmation, and feature-root scoping behavior.
Any required supporting no-regression assertions in orchestration-facing tests are added only where needed.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `tests/test_tui.py` with three regression tests covering manual-vs-auto refresh defaults, deterministic mouse routing across list/detail container widgets, and no-op panel updates unless content changes or detail refresh is forced. Ran `python -m unittest tests.test_tui` successfully (55 tests, passing). Existing orchestrator safety-path coverage was already sufficient, so `tests/test_orchestrator.py` did not need changes.
handoff_summary.remaining: No unresolved tester-scope work remains for this bead.
handoff_summary.risks: `python -m pytest tests/test_tui.py tests/test_orchestrator.py` could not be used because `pytest` is not installed in the current environment; validation used `unittest` instead.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed with downstream review or integration using the added TUI regression coverage.

execution_history.summary: Added TUI regression tests for default manual refresh mode, container-based mouse routing with detail-scroll reset semantics, and incremental panel rerender caching. Targeted TUI validation passed under `unittest`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run targeted checks and summarize failures clearly.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes only when strictly necessary to execute or stabilize the tests.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
